---
title: "Internalizing Environmental Risk: Insurance Design and Firm Behavior in Hazardous Industries"
subtitle: ""
author: "Kaleb K. Javier"
format:
  beamer:
    template: "my-template.tex"
    keep-tex: true
    aspectratio: 169
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    header-includes:
      - |
        \providecommand{\estc}[1]{\textcolor[HTML]{1B7837}{#1}}
theme-bg-color: "0D2D52"
---







##

\LightMode

```{=latex}
\begin{columns}[c]
\begin{column}{0.5\textwidth}
\centering
\includegraphics[width=\linewidth,height=0.8\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Data/tank_jpg.jpg}
\end{column}
\begin{column}{0.5\textwidth}
\centering
\includegraphics[width=\linewidth,height=0.8\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Data/LUST.jpg}
\end{column}
\end{columns}
```

---


## Compulsory insurance works only if it prices risk

\BerkeleyLightMode

- U.S. environmental law makes polluters pay for the harm they cause: strict liability (OPA 1990, SMCRA 1977, CERCLA 1980, RCRA 1976).
- It also requires proof they can pay: bonding, minimum assets, or compulsory insurance.
- When that proof is insurance, how the insurance is priced decides whether the firm has any reason to cut risk.

\vfill
\centering\textbf{This paper examines the design and efficacy of compulsory liability insurance as a financial responsibility instrument, using underground storage tanks (USTs) as a case study for environmentally hazardous settings.}
---


## (UST) leaks (LUSTs) are common, costly, and borne by others

\BerkeleyLightMode

- About 1 in 4 facilities leaks over a 30-year life, as high as oil and gas wells.
- USTs are the top source of US groundwater contamination; petroleum and benzene reach drinking-water wells (EPA 2024).
- Cleanups are costly: median about \$136k, mean about \$403k per site (my claims data).
- Contamination reaches drinking water and lowers infant birthweight (Marcus 2021).
- The public funds most states use to cover cleanups are winding down: more than a dozen of the 37 fund states are set to close theirs this decade (ASTSWMO State Fund Survey 2025).

\vfill
\centering\textit{Leak risk is not random: it rises with tank age and construction, both of which an insurer can observe and price.}

---




## A clean natural experiment: Texas, 1998

\BerkeleyLightMode

- Every US gas station stores fuel underground: about 140,000 facilities, 550,000 tanks (EPA 2024).
- Since 1988, every owner must carry at least \$1M in coverage (RCRA Subtitle I).
- Most states use a \textbf{state-run, uniform premium}: everyone pays the same regardless of risk, so owners have no reason to retire old tanks.
- \textbf{Texas, December 22, 1998:} the fund closed overnight. About 26,000 facilities moved to \textbf{risk-rated private insurance}, driven by the fund's insolvency, not by any change in Texas tanks.

---


## This work answers 3 questions

\BerkeleyLightMode

\textbf{Q1. Observability.} Is release risk predictable from observable tank characteristics? If not, the premium schedule is noise, not signal.

\vspace{0.2cm}

\textbf{Q2. Behavior.} Does risk-based pricing change closure, replacement, and release outcomes?

\vspace{0.2cm}

\textbf{Q3. Welfare.} How much of the first-best welfare gain does risk-rated pricing capture?

\vfill
\centering\textit{Three answers, one argument: risk is priceable (Q1), so pricing it moves behavior (Q2), and that behavior change recovers part, not all, of the lost welfare (Q3).}

---


## What am are doing this summer at PERC

\BerkeleyLightMode

\textbf{1. Who responds, and what it does to local markets (reduced form).}

- Heterogeneity by geography (urban vs rural) and firm type (branded vs independent): which firms exit, which replace
- Whether the reform raised or lowered local market concentration

\vspace{0.25cm}

\textbf{2. Cross-subsidies under the uniform premium (reduced form).}

- Use the claims data to estimate each facility's actuarially fair premium, then compare it to what it pays
- Measure how much low-risk facilities subsidize high-risk ones under a uniform premium

\vspace{0.25cm}

\textbf{3. The structural model.}

- Estimate the portfolio model, then run the four counterfactuals and deliver the welfare comparison

---


## More Details

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{slide-roadmap}{}
```

\vspace{0.2cm}

\begin{center}
\large
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.7}
\begin{tabular}{ll}
\textbf{Section} & \textbf{Jump to} \\
\hline
Data                  & \navbutton[slide-data][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\small, inner sep=4pt]{Data $\rightarrow$} \\[4pt]
Empirical Approach    & \navbutton[slide-rf][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\small, inner sep=4pt]{Empirical Approach $\rightarrow$} \\[4pt]
Results               & \navbutton[slide-results][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\small, inner sep=4pt]{Results $\rightarrow$} \\[4pt]
Structural Model      & \navbutton[slide-structural][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\small, inner sep=4pt]{Structural Model $\rightarrow$} \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

---













##

\BerkeleyMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{slide-data}{}
```

::: {.layout-statement}
\begin{center}
\Huge \textbf{Data}
\end{center}
:::

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Data: three collection efforts, 18 usable states

\BerkeleyLightMode

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{State + EPA administrative records} --- UST inventory and confirmed releases from all 50 states and EPA LUST Finder; tank-level attributes (install date, wall type, piping, leak detection) and cleanup dispositions
  \hfill \navbutton[baseline-table][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{Summary statistics $\rightarrow$}

  \vspace{0.25cm}

  \item \textbf{Cleanup cost data} --- Trust-fund claims from 6 control states; first multistate per-site cleanup cost study; median \$136k, mean \$403k; right tail dominates expected loss
  \hfill \navbutton[cost-dist-pooled][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{Claims distribution $\rightarrow$}

  \vspace{0.25cm}

  \item \textbf{TX insurance rate filings} --- Mid-Continent Casualty SERFF filings, 2006--2024; reconstructs the risk-based premium schedule $P(s)$ at the tank level from base rates, ILFs, and attribute credits/debits
  \hfill \navbutton[premium-divergence][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{Premium figure $\rightarrow$}
\end{itemize}

\vspace{0.2cm}
\textbf{Why 18 states?} 20 of 38 flat-fee states dropped: missing closure dates, install dates, or wall construction variables. The 17 controls plus Texas have complete records across all three dimensions.

---


\LightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{baseline-table}{}
\vspace{0.1cm}
\input{../../Output/Tables/T_Baseline_Characteristics_Slide.tex}
```

\vspace{0.1cm}
\centering\scriptsize\textit{18 study states; alive-at-reform = facilities with $\geq 1$ tank active on Dec 22, 1998. Wall, vintage, and age computed over tanks active at reform; profile averages over all study facility-years. Capacity is the median (robust to large-facility outliers).}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[baseline-tanks][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{Tank-size distribution $\rightarrow$}%
  \hspace{3pt}%
  \navbutton[baseline-cap][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{Capacity distribution $\rightarrow$}%
};
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-data][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Data}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Releases are costly to cleanup budgets and human health

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{cost-dist-pooled}{}
```

\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[height=0.72\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/Figure_cost_distribution_pooled.png}
\end{center}

\vfill
\centering\small\textit{$\sim$\,550,000 confirmed releases since 1988. Median cleanup \$136k; mean \$403k. Right tail dominates expected loss.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[cost-dist-states][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{Claims by state $\rightarrow$}%
};
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-data][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Data}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Cleanup cost distribution by state (6 trust-fund states) {visibility="uncounted"}

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{cost-dist-states}{}
```

\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.92\textwidth,height=0.72\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/Figure_cost_distribution_by_state.png}
\end{center}

\vfill
\centering\small\textit{Per-site cleanup cost by state, deflated to 2023 dollars. State-level variation in the cost distribution; pooled distribution in prior slide masks meaningful right-tail heterogeneity.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[cost-dist-pooled][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Pooled distribution}%
};
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-data][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Data}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Premium divergence: Texas vs. control states (1992--2020)

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{premium-divergence}{}
```

\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.92\textwidth,height=0.78\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/04e_Premium_by_Year_Treatment.png}
\end{center}

\vspace{0.1cm}
\centering\small\textit{Flat and parallel before 1998; sharp Texas divergence after the reform. Sets up the DiD without showing it.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-data][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Data}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Risk is predictable: the age-hazard gradient (setup)

\BerkeleyLightMode

**Estimand.** Annual first-release hazard in the never-yet-leaked risk set:

$$
h(t \mid x) \;=\; P\bigl(\,\text{first confirmed release in year } t \mid \text{no prior release}, \; x\,\bigr)
$$

In plain words: given what an underwriter can observe at the start of a policy year, what's the probability of a claim?

**Model.** Penalized logistic regression on the pre-treatment panel:

- $x$: 3-year age bins fully interacted with wall construction; state FE; fuel and portfolio controls
- Lasso variable selection; inverse-frequency weights for the 0.70\% base rate
- Out-of-sample CV (10 folds); post-hoc recalibration to the empirical event rate

**Sample.** 3.93M pre-treatment facility-years; $27{,}455$ confirmed first-release events.

$\hat h(x)$ feeds the next figure and the structural-model hazard $h(a_t, W_i)$.

---


## Single-walled tanks drive the age-hazard gradient

\BerkeleyLightMode

\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.92\textwidth,height=0.78\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/Figure_CV_CellRisk.png}
\end{center}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[cell-gof][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{Goodness of fit $\rightarrow$}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## The insurance market: how Texas premiums are built

\BerkeleyLightMode

**Texas** --- rebuilt from major Texas UST insurer's rate filings (SERFF, 2006--2024):

$$P_{ijt} \;=\; \text{Base}_t \;\times\; \text{ILF}_{it} \;\times\; \Bigl(1 \;+\; \sum_{k} \lambda_{k,t}\, X_{ijt,k}\Bigr)$$

- $\text{Base}_t$: per-tank base rate, four filing periods
- $\text{ILF}_{it}$: increased-limit factor; fix coverage at federal-minimum \$1M/\$1M
- $X_{ijt}$: tank age, wall, piping, leak-detection technology
- $\lambda_{k,t}$: additive credits/debits from the filed schedule

**Control states** --- state financial-responsibility fund records: a per-tank annual assessment, largely flat across years and tank characteristics.

---


## Premiums track the risk we can predict

\BerkeleyLightMode

\vspace{0.1cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[height=0.80\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/Figure_Premium_vs_Hazard_Raw.png}
\end{center}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[premium-vs-el][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{Predicted per-tank EL $\rightarrow$}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---




##

\BerkeleyMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{slide-rf}{}
```

::: {.layout-statement}
\begin{center}
\Huge \textbf{Empirical Approach}
\end{center}
:::

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Research design: DiD with facility FE and cell-by-year FE

\BerkeleyLightMode

**Static DiD.**

$$Y_{it} \;=\; \beta\,(\text{Texas}_{f(i)} \times \text{Post}_t) \;+\; \alpha_i \;+\; \delta_{ct} \;+\; \varepsilon_{it}$$

**Event study** (replaces single Post indicator with per-year coefficients relative to 1998):

$$Y_{it} \;=\; \sum_{\tau \neq -1} \beta_\tau \cdot \bigl(\text{Texas}_{f(i)} \times \mathbf{1}[t - 1998 = \tau]\bigr) \;+\; \alpha_i \;+\; \delta_{ct} \;+\; \varepsilon_{it}$$

- $\alpha_i$: facility FE. $\delta_{ct}$: cell$\times$year FE with $c = \{\text{wall}\times\text{fuel}\times\text{capacity}\times\text{install cohort}\}$
- *Identifying variation:* within a single tank-vintage cell, in the same calendar year, $\hat\beta$ is the change in the Texas-vs-control closure-rate gap before vs.\ after Dec 22, 1998
- *Inference:* one treated unit $\rightarrow$ Conley-Taber (2011) placebo inference; wild-cluster bootstrap at state level (18 clusters) as cross-check

\vspace{0.2cm}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---





##

\BerkeleyMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{slide-results}{}
```

::: {.layout-statement}
\begin{center}
\Huge \textbf{Results}
\end{center}
:::

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Event study: closure response in Texas

\BerkeleyLightMode

\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.92\textwidth,height=0.78\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/Fig_ES_Full.pdf}
\end{center}

\vspace{0.1cm}
\centering\small\textit{Pre-trends flat; sharp break at Dec 22, 1998; sustained post-treatment effect.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## ATT on Tank Closure: $+1.58$ pp on a $2.0\%$ Baseline

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\vspace{0.15cm}
\begin{center}
\begingroup
\scriptsize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{5pt}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{0.95}
\input{../../Output/Tables/T_DiD_Stepped_Full.tex}
\endgroup
\end{center}
```

\vfill

\centering
\textit{Cols add facility FE, then cell$\times$year FE. Col (3) is the headline.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Heterogeneity: Pre-89 Vintage Drives the Effect

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\vspace{-0.1cm}
\begin{center}
\begingroup
\scriptsize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{0.7}
\input{../../Output/Tables/T_HTE_DiD_Vintage.tex}
\endgroup
\end{center}
```

\centering\scriptsize
\textit{Pre-89 ATT $= \hat\beta_0 + \hat\beta_1 \approx +3.1$ pp; post-88 ATT $= \hat\beta_0 \approx +0.6$ pp. A coincident Texas-wide shock predicts uniform response across vintage; only the insurance premium gradient predicts the 5$\times$ differential.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[vintage-forest][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{ATT by install year $\rightarrow$}%
};
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## LUST discovery falls under risk-based pricing

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\begingroup
\footnotesize
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.05}
\input{../../Output/Tables/T_LUST_Incumbent_Slide.tex}
\endgroup
\end{center}
```

\vspace{0.1cm}
\centering\small\textit{Leak discovery falls $1.27$ pp on a $1.73\%$ base; both routine-monitoring and closure-inspection findings decline.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Closure composition shifts toward replacement

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\vspace{0.05cm}
\begin{center}
\begingroup
\let\small\scriptsize
\scriptsize
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{0.85}
\input{../../Output/Tables/T2b_Enriched_Slide.tex}
\endgroup
\end{center}
```

\vspace{0.05cm}
\centering\scriptsize\textit{Among closing facilities, RBP raises the replacement (upgrade) share and lowers permanent closures --- the margin the structural model recovers.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---






## What the reduced form cannot tell us

\BerkeleyLightMode

\textbf{The DiD identifies an average treatment effect. Three questions it cannot answer:}

\vspace{0.2cm}

\begin{enumerate}

\item \textbf{How large are the welfare effects in dollar terms?} The DiD gives a closure-rate gap, not a present-value welfare number on which to compare policies.

\item \textbf{What would alternative policies (subsidy, Pigouvian surcharge, age mandate) actually do?} These were not implemented in Texas. Their welfare consequences are counterfactual.

\item \textbf{How far are we from first-best, and why?} Risk-based pricing is a partial fix: premiums price what the insurer can claim, but health damages are structurally unpriced. Quantifying the residual wedge needs the structural primitives.

\end{enumerate}

\vfill

\textbf{Recovering the structural parameters is the only way to answer these. The structural model is not an add-on, it is required.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-structural][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{Structural model $\rightarrow$}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


##

\BerkeleyMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{slide-structural}{}
```

::: {.layout-statement}
\begin{center}
\Huge \textbf{Structural Model \& Counterfactuals}
\end{center}
:::

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## What a facility looks like in the model

\BerkeleyLightMode

- A facility is a set of underground tanks. Two things about each tank drive its cost and its risk: how old it is, and whether it is single- or double-walled.
- The model describes a facility by the mix of tanks it holds: how many fall into each age-and-wall group, how much fuel it can store, and which pricing regime it faces.
- Each year the owner decides what to do with that set: keep it as is, close some tanks, add new ones, or shut down for good.

\vfill
\centering\small\textit{Tanks are retired and replaced one at a time, so the real choice is how many to close or add, not a single keep-or-quit decision.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## State: the portfolio. Action: how many tanks to close or add

\BerkeleyLightMode

$$s = (\,n,\; G,\; \rho\,)$$

- $n$: counts of tanks across 16 categories: wall type (single or double) $\times$ eight 5-year age bins
- $G$: facility capacity bin; $\rho$: pricing regime, fixed for the facility's life
- A large but tractable state space, about \textbf{298{,}000} states in all

\vspace{0.15cm}

\textbf{Action} $(k,m)$: close $k$ tanks, install $m$. \quad $(0,0)$ maintain $\;\cdot\;$ $(k,0)$ downsize $\;\cdot\;$ $(k,m\ge 1)$ replace $\;\cdot\;$ $X$ exit.

- \textbf{Which} tanks leave is a rule, not a choice: oldest single-walled first. New tanks are double-walled (law).

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[model-coding][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{How a facility-year is coded $\rightarrow$}%
};
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Each option's payoff: measured prices in, estimated weights out

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\begin{align*}
u_{(k,m)}(s) &= \varphi_G + \alpha_g - \estc{\gamma_p}\, P(n',\rho) - \estc{\gamma_r}\, OOP(n') - \estc{c^{\text{rem}}}\,k - \estc{c^{\text{inst}}}\,m &&\text{\small(operate)}\\[3pt]
u_X(s) &= \estc{\kappa_1}\, N(n) &&\text{\small(exit)}
\end{align*}
```

\vspace{0.1cm}

- \textbf{Measured first, not estimated:} the premium bill $P$ (Texas filings or state fee), the leak hazard $H$, out-of-pocket $OOP = H \cdot D$, and the aging and capacity transitions
- \textbf{Estimated} (in \estc{green}): $\estc{\varphi_G}$ operating payoff by size, $\estc{\gamma_p}$ premium weight, $\estc{\gamma_r}$ out-of-pocket weight, $\estc{c^{\text{rem}}}$ and $\estc{c^{\text{inst}}}$ close / install costs, $\estc{\kappa_1}$ exit value per tank
- $\estc{\gamma_p} = \estc{\gamma_r}$ is a testable restriction: does a premium dollar feel like an expected out-of-pocket dollar?

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## The dynamic choice: today's payoff vs. operating next year

\BerkeleyLightMode

$$v_a(s) = u_a(s) + \beta\,\mathbb{E}\!\left[V(s') \mid s, a\right], \qquad V(s) = \log\!\Bigl(\, e^{v_{\text{maintain}}(s)} + e^{IV(s)} + e^{v_{\text{exit}}(s)} \,\Bigr)$$

\vspace{0.1cm}

- Each year the owner compares \textbf{maintain}, any \textbf{tank-work} option $(k,m)$, and \textbf{exit}, trading today's payoff against the value of operating next year
- Nested logit: the tank-work options share a draw $\lambda$ for the common decision to touch the tanks at all
- $\beta = 0.95$; estimated by nested pseudo-likelihood (Aguirregabiria \& Mira 2007). Eight economic parameters plus state fixed effects

\vfill
\centering\small\textit{Risk-based pricing makes the premium climb with age, so the value of holding old tanks falls and owners close or replace sooner.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Why risk pricing is only a partial fix

\BerkeleyLightMode

- A leak costs society $L + E$: cleanup $L$ plus external health and water damage $E$ beyond cleanup
- The owner pays only the deductible $D$ directly. The rest of cleanup, $L - D$, falls on the insurer or fund. The external damage $E$ is borne by no one in the market.

\vspace{0.15cm}
\begin{center}
\small
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.3}
\begin{tabular}{lll}
\hline
\textbf{Regime} & \textbf{Owner internalizes} & \textbf{Wedge left open} \\
\hline
Uniform premium & roughly only the deductible $D$           & $(L - D) + E$ \\
Risk-based & $D$ plus part of $L - D$                  & $E$ plus the missing part of $L - D$ \\
First-best & the full $H\,(L + E)$ at the margin        & none \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\vfill
\centering\small\textit{Social welfare $=$ producer surplus $-$ external damage $-$ government outlay. $E$ calibrated at \$50k per leak, with a band.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Four counterfactuals (estimation in progress, no results yet)

\BerkeleyLightMode

Once the portfolio model is estimated, we run four policy experiments:

\vspace{0.2cm}

\begin{enumerate}
\item \textbf{Uniform-premium Texas.} Texas keeps its state-run uniform premium. The welfare cost of the reform itself.
\item \textbf{First-best Pigouvian contract.} Exposure set to the full social cost $H(L+E)$. The welfare ceiling.
\item \textbf{Single-walled replacement subsidy.} Pay down the cost of replacing the riskiest tanks.
\item \textbf{Age mandate.} Force removal at age $A$.
\end{enumerate}

\vfill
\centering\small\textit{Each is judged by the margins it moves: closure, replacement, leaks, and welfare. No results yet; this is the plan for the summer.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south east, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=-10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south east) {%
  \navbutton[slide-roadmap][fill=BerkeleyBlue!60, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ Roadmap}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---




## Appendix: Cell-Level Goodness of Fit {visibility="uncounted"}

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{cell-gof}{}
```

\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.92\textwidth,height=0.78\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/Figure_CV_CellRisk_GoF.png}
\end{center}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[slide-data][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ back}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Appendix: ATT by installation vintage {visibility="uncounted"}

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{vintage-forest}{}
```

\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.92\textwidth,height=0.78\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/Fig_Vintage_Forest.pdf}
\end{center}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[slide-results][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ back}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Appendix: Premium vs. Per-Tank Expected Loss {visibility="uncounted"}

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{premium-vs-el}{}
```

\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.92\textwidth,height=0.78\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/Figure_Premium_vs_EL_Raw.png}
\end{center}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[slide-data][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ back}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---




## Appendix: Facility size at reform --- Texas vs.\ controls {visibility="uncounted"}

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{baseline-tanks}{}
```

\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.92\textwidth,height=0.78\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/Fig_Baseline_TanksAtReform.png}
\end{center}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[baseline-table][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ back}%
  \hspace{3pt}%
  \navbutton[baseline-cap][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{Capacity $\rightarrow$}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Appendix: Facility capacity at reform --- Texas vs.\ controls {visibility="uncounted"}

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{baseline-cap}{}
```

\vspace{0.2cm}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.92\textwidth,height=0.78\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../../Output/Figures/Fig_Baseline_CapacityAtReform.png}
\end{center}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[baseline-table][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ back}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---


## Appendix: How the model codes each facility-year {visibility="uncounted"}

\BerkeleyLightMode

```{=latex}
\hypertarget{model-coding}{}
```

Let $k_{it}$ = tanks closed and $m_{it}$ = tanks installed in year $t$. Each facility-year gets exactly one action, by precedence:

```{=latex}
\begin{align*}
\textbf{Exit } X         &: \quad \text{every tank closed} \\
\textbf{Replace } (k,m\ge 1) &: \quad k_{it}\ge 1,\;\; m_{it}\ge 1,\;\; \text{still operating} \\
\textbf{Downsize } (k,0) &: \quad k_{it}\ge 1,\;\; m_{it}=0,\;\; \text{still operating} \\
\textbf{Maintain } (0,0) &: \quad k_{it}=0
\end{align*}
```

\vspace{0.1cm}

- **State** coded from the beginning-of-year portfolio: counts $n$ over 16 (wall $\times$ age) cells, capacity bin $G$, regime $\rho$
- **Which tanks leave** is the rule $r(n,k)$ --- oldest single-walled first --- *not* a choice; only the counts $k,\,m$ are chosen
- The new tank enters double-walled at age bin 1 (regulation)

\vfill
\centering\small\textit{Downsizing --- coded ``maintain'' or ``exit'' in the single-tank model --- is now its own action.}

```{=latex}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=south west, inner sep=0pt] at ([xshift=10pt, yshift=10pt]current page.south west) {%
  \navbutton[slide-structural][fill=BerkeleyBlue, font=\tiny, inner sep=2pt]{$\leftarrow$ back}%
};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

---




## Texas was first. The state-run uniform-premium model is winding down nationwide {visibility="uncounted"}

\BerkeleyLightMode

- Six states have already replaced their state-run uniform premium with private, risk-rated coverage: Arizona, Connecticut, Florida, New York, Texas, Wisconsin.
- More than a dozen others carry a statutory fund sunset (claims-bar) date. The dates cluster through this decade:

\vspace{0.15cm}
\begin{center}
\small
Minnesota 2022 \quad Colorado 2023 \quad Kansas / Nebraska 2024 \\[3pt]
Illinois / New Hampshire / Missouri 2025 \quad California / South Carolina 2026 \\[3pt]
Utah 2028 \quad Vermont 2029 \quad Washington 2030 \quad Oklahoma 2032
\end{center}
\vspace{0.1cm}

- The gasoline fees that bankroll these programs are in structural decline as fuel use shifts (ASTSWMO 2023).

\vfill
\centering\small\textit{This paper measures the welfare consequences of exactly the transition many states are now facing. (California's 2026 date was pushed to 2036 in 2024, so these sunsets get re-litigated.) Source: ASTSWMO State Fund Survey.}
